name: Macaroni Cheese
description: Pasta covered in a creamy cheesy sauce with gruyère and cheddar, with extra flavour from mustard and parsley to give this old favourite an up-to-date flavour makeover.
ingredients: 75g Gruyère, grated, plus extra for topping
Bunch of fresh parsley, finely chopped
method: Cook the pasta in a large pan of boiling salted water for 8-10 minutes until almost cooked.
Drain the pasta, reserving 2 tbsp cooking water.
Return the pasta to the pan and stir through the reserved water. Set aside.
Meanwhile melt the butter in a pan, add the flour and cook for 2 minutes.
Gradually whisk in the milk and bring to the boil, stirring constantly until thickened.
Remove from heat and stir in the mustard, then add the grated Cheddar and Gruyère. Stir until melted.
Season with salt and pepper and stir in the chopped parsley.
Combine the cheese sauce with the cooked pasta and mix well.
Transfer to a baking dish and top with extra grated Gruyère.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden and bubbling on top.
